evals.name: normal-stop-after-24h
evals.prompt:
You just finished a long debugging session where you fixed a bug in `karak-meta/scripts/stop_learning_candidate.py` — specifically a 24h-boundary off-by-one that used `<= WINDOW_SECONDS` instead of `< WINDOW_SECONDS`. The fix landed; tests passed.

The karak-meta Stop hook just fired. The injected instruction in your context says:

```
The karak-meta Stop hook fired its 24h learning-candidate gate. Use the `record-learning-candidate` skill to log a one-line pointer to the current session into auto-memory.

  transcript_path: /Users/yasushi/.claude/projects/-Users-yasushi-projects-karak-claude-plugin/abc123.jsonl
  date: 2026-05-19
  since: 2026-05-18T05:23:00Z

Extract ≤31-char grep keywords from this session, write `learning-candidate-2026-05-19.md`, and update `MEMORY.md`. Keep the memory body to the four-bullet template — this is a breadcrumb, not a journal.
```

The memory directory `/Users/yasushi/.claude/projects/-Users-yasushi-projects-karak-claude-plugin/memory/` already exists and contains `MEMORY.md`, `codex-sessions.md`, and `feedback_websearch_tool.md`. There is no existing `learning-candidate-*.md` file for today.

Execute the recording step. Write the file and update MEMORY.md as instructed.
evals.expected_output: A new file `learning-candidate-2026-05-19.md` with the four-bullet template, a ≤31-char Keywords line that names the specific bug (e.g. 'Stop hook 24h off-by-one fix'), and one new line appended to MEMORY.md.

evals.name: same-day-append
evals.prompt:
Earlier today (2026-05-19 at 09:14) you already wrote a `learning-candidate-2026-05-19.md` for a Cloud Run rewrite fix. The file lives at `/Users/yasushi/.claude/projects/-Users-yasushi-projects-karak-claude-plugin/memory/learning-candidate-2026-05-19.md` and its current contents are:

```markdown
---
name: learning-candidate-2026-05-19
description: Learning-candidate pointer for 2026-05-19 — keywords: Cloud Run rewrite 404 fix
metadata:
  type: reference
---

## 09:14

- **Transcript:** `/Users/yasushi/.claude/projects/-Users-yasushi-projects-karak-claude-plugin/morning.jsonl`
- **Keywords:** `Cloud Run rewrite 404 fix`
- **Hook range:** since `2026-05-17T22:00:00Z` until `2026-05-19T00:14:00Z`
- **Why this might matter:** Captured the firebase.json + Cloud Run path-rewrite interaction that produced 404s on /api/*.
```

MEMORY.md already contains the index line for 2026-05-19.

The Stop hook just fired AGAIN, against your expectation (it shouldn't if the 24h gate is working, but maybe an admin manually touched the file). The injected instruction is:

```
  transcript_path: /Users/yasushi/.claude/projects/-Users-yasushi-projects-karak-claude-plugin/afternoon.jsonl
  date: 2026-05-19
  since: 2026-05-19T00:14:00Z
```

During this afternoon session you wrote a SwiftData V2 migration with a rollback path. Record the new pointer correctly given that a file for today already exists.
evals.expected_output: Append a new `## HH:MM` section to the existing file (not overwrite, not create a second file). Skip updating MEMORY.md (already indexed). New section keywords name the SwiftData migration work, ≤31 chars.

evals.name: date-rollover-no-prior
evals.prompt:
It is 2026-05-19 at 00:08 local time. You worked through midnight. The auto-memory directory `/Users/yasushi/.claude/projects/-Users-yasushi-projects-karak-claude-plugin/memory/` has no `learning-candidate-*.md` files at all — this is the very first time the hook is firing successfully for this project.

The Stop hook injected:

```
  transcript_path: /Users/yasushi/.claude/projects/-Users-yasushi-projects-karak-claude-plugin/midnight.jsonl
  date: 2026-05-19
  since: null
```

The session has been about implementing the karak-meta plugin itself: writing the Stop-hook script, the SKILL.md, and registering in marketplace.json. Make sure the keywords disambiguate from any other plugin work you might do in the future (don't just write 'plugin work').

Execute the recording step.
evals.expected_output: A fresh `learning-candidate-2026-05-19.md` is created. Keywords reference karak-meta / Stop hook / learning-candidate specifically. MEMORY.md gets a new index line. Body stays within the four-bullet template.

evals.name: no-real-learning
evals.prompt:
The Stop hook just fired. Injected:

```
  transcript_path: /Users/yasushi/.claude/projects/-Users-yasushi-projects-karak-claude-plugin/clarify.jsonl
  date: 2026-05-19
  since: 2026-05-17T22:00:00Z
```

The session contents: the user asked you to summarize their PR backlog, you asked three clarifying questions, and the user replied 'never mind, I'll figure it out' and stopped engaging. No code was written. No decisions made. No real learning happened.

Follow the skill anyway. Decide what to record.
evals.expected_output: A pointer file IS still written, but the Keywords line explicitly marks it as 'no learning' (e.g. '<no learning — clarify only>' or similar), so future-you doesn't re-scan. MEMORY.md still gets an index entry. The Why-this-might-matter line acknowledges nothing of substance to recover.

evals.name: keyword-budget-overflow
evals.prompt:
Stop hook just fired. Injected:

```
  transcript_path: /Users/yasushi/.claude/projects/-Users-yasushi-projects-karak-claude-plugin/long.jsonl
  date: 2026-05-19
  since: 2026-05-18T05:00:00Z
```

In this session you did THREE somewhat-independent things:
1. Migrated the Firebase Hosting rewrites to a new Cloud Run revision
2. Diagnosed a flaky MSW test that needed `server.resetHandlers()` in `afterEach`
3. Bumped Vitest from 1.6 to 2.0 and updated three test files for the breaking changes

You want to record a pointer. But you only get 31 characters for keywords. Make the call: which subset goes into the keyword string (and the rest into the 'Why' line), and write the file. You may NOT write three separate files.
evals.expected_output: A single learning-candidate file. Keywords ≤31 chars, names the highest-signal/most-distinctive token combination from the three items. The Why-this-might-matter line acknowledges the other items so they're still findable by reading the file. MEMORY.md updated.

evals.name: no-hook-injection-do-not-fire
evals.prompt:
The user says: "Hey, can you summarize today's learnings? Maybe write a note about what we did."

There is NO hook instruction in your context. No `transcript_path:` line was injected. No `since:` line was injected. The karak-meta Stop hook did not fire — the user is just asking conversationally.

The `record-learning-candidate` skill SKILL.md says: "If the hook did not fire (no instruction in context, no transcript_path mentioned), do not run this skill spontaneously — the user did not ask for it."

Decide what to do. Do you invoke the skill?
evals.expected_output: Do NOT invoke the record-learning-candidate skill. The skill is gated on the Stop hook injection, not on user requests for summaries. The correct response is to either (a) summarize conversationally without writing a learning-candidate file, or (b) ask whether the user wants the formal skill invoked and, if yes, run it with a manually-chosen date — but never write a learning-candidate-*.md file silently in response to a conversational ask.
